company_type: Technology - Internet Services and Cloud Computing

manufacturing_locations: Multiple global locations for data centers
Contract manufacturers for servers and devices
dependencies: Contract manufacturers for assembly of servers and devices
Single or limited source suppliers for certain technical infrastructure components
Industry supply capacity for AI accelerators including GPUs and custom-built TPUs is highly competitive
risks: Supply shortages and price increases driven by raw material/component availability
Manufacturing capacity constraints and labor shortages
Tariffs, sanctions, export controls, and trade disputes
Forced labor concerns in supply chain
Geopolitical tensions and armed conflicts affecting supply
Natural disasters, pandemics, and climate change effects (sea level rise, drought, flooding, heat waves, wildfires, power shutdowns)
Quality issues and product recalls from suppliers
Single-source or limited-source supplier dependencies for technical infrastructure components
Competitive supply constraints for AI accelerators (GPUs, TPUs)

regulatory_risks: Antitrust laws and competition enforcement (U.S. DOJ lawsuits, European Commission decisions, investigations in Australia, Japan, India, UK)
Digital Markets Act (EU) compliance requirements
Data privacy and security regulations (GDPR, UK GDPR, U.S. state privacy laws including California, Illinois, Texas, Washington, New York)
Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) and state children's privacy laws
AI regulation (EU AI Act, U.S. federal and state AI regulations, deepfakes legislation)
Copyright and intellectual property laws (EU Directive on Copyright in the Digital Single Market)
Content moderation laws (Digital Services Act, Florida SB 7072, Texas HB 20, Singapore, Australia, UK regulations)
Online child safety regulations
Consumer protection laws (EU New Deal for Consumers)
Section 230 of Communications Decency Act and DMCA safe harbor protections
Environmental sustainability and climate change regulations
Export controls and sanctions compliance
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-corruption laws
Tax regulations including OECD minimum global tax rate initiatives
Digital services taxes and revenue-based taxes in various jurisdictions
Google Play billing and fee regulations (South Korea and other jurisdictions)
News publication payment requirements
Data localization and data transfer restrictions
Biometric information collection laws
Immigration policy affecting talent mobility

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance affecting operations and customer access
Export controls limiting ability to operate in certain jurisdictions or offer products/services
Russia-related sanctions and ongoing legal matters
Potential sanctions affecting supply chain and component sourcing
Market access barriers due to political unrest and geopolitical tensions
Armed conflicts in Middle East and Ukraine affecting business continuity
